skill.name: lark-sheets
skill.description: Create and manage Lark/Feishu spreadsheets. Create spreadsheets, read and write cell values, append rows, find cells within known spreadsheets, and export spreadsheet files.
skill.summary: Official Lark/Feishu spreadsheet skill for creating, reading, writing, and managing spreadsheet data with full API support.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 374 patterns that are FALSE POSITIVE. The skill consists of markdown documentation showing CLI command examples. Backticks denote inline code references (e.g., `lark-cli sheets +append`), not shell execution. Path traversal patterns are markdown relative links to other documentation files. URLs are example endpoints for user reference. The skill requires lark-cli binary and Lark API authentication - no malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - External Commands
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 287 backtick occurrences. These are markdown inline code formatting for CLI documentation, not actual shell command execution. Example: `lark-cli sheets +append` shows command usage syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backtick content is documentation showing CLI command examples. No eval, exec, spawn, or actual shell execution exists in these markdown files.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Path Traversal
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 39 path traversal patterns. These are markdown relative links to other documentation files (e.g., `../../lark-shared/SKILL.md`), not actual filesystem path traversal attacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All '../' patterns are markdown link syntax for cross-referencing documentation files within the skill repository.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Network URLs
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 16 hardcoded URLs. These are example URLs in documentation showing users the expected Lark/Feishu URL format (e.g., https://example.larksuite.com/sheets/shtcnxxxxx).
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs use placeholder/example values (example.larksuite.com) and serve as documentation for users to understand URL format patterns.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - C2 and Reconnaissance Keywords
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged C2 keywords and system reconnaissance patterns. These are false matches on legitimate API terminology (spreadsheet, token, sheet_id, etc.) in API documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Terms like 'spreadsheet', 'token', and API method names triggered keyword-based detection. Context confirms legitimate Lark API documentation.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Cryptographic/Entropy Patterns
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged weak cryptographic algorithm and high entropy strings. These are base64-encoded placeholder tokens (e.g., 'doxcnxxxxxxxxx', 'shtcnxxxxxxxxx') used as examples in documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: High entropy strings are placeholder token examples for documentation. No actual cryptographic operations or obfuscated payloads exist.

content.value_statement: Working with Lark/Feishu spreadsheets requires handling multiple API operations and authentication. This skill provides ready-to-use commands for creating, reading, writing, and managing spreadsheet data without manual API calls.
actual_capabilities: Create new spreadsheets with optional headers and initial data rows
Read spreadsheet cell values and retrieve sheet information
Write data to spreadsheet cells in overwrite mode
Append new rows to existing spreadsheets
Find cells by content within known spreadsheets
Export spreadsheets to files with async task polling
limitations: Requires lark-cli binary to be installed and configured with valid Lark API credentials
Cannot search cloud space by spreadsheet name - use lark-doc skill first to find files
All spreadsheet operations require valid spreadsheet token or URL
Some operations like filters require understanding of Lark API parameter structures

use_cases.title: Automated Reporting Dashboards
use_cases.description: Create and maintain weekly or monthly reporting spreadsheets by automatically appending new data rows as they become available.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts and operations managers who track KPIs in Lark spreadsheets

use_cases.title: Collaborative Data Collection
use_cases.description: Set up shared spreadsheets for team data collection, create headers, and programmatically write structured data from various sources.
use_cases.target_user: Project coordinators managing team inputs across departments

use_cases.title: Spreadsheet Data Migration
use_cases.description: Export existing spreadsheets, read data from source files, and write to new spreadsheets with transformed or consolidated data.
use_cases.target_user: IT administrators migrating data between Lark workspaces

prompt_templates.title: Create a spreadsheet with headers
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Lark spreadsheet titled "Inventory Tracker" with columns: Item Name, SKU, Quantity, Price, Last Updated. Add an initial row with sample data.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Basic spreadsheet creation with initial data

prompt_templates.title: Read and analyze spreadsheet data
prompt_templates.prompt: Read all data from the spreadsheet at [URL]. Find all cells containing values greater than 10000 in column C and summarize the results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Reading and finding data in existing spreadsheets

prompt_templates.title: Append data to existing spreadsheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Append the following sales data to the spreadsheet "Monthly Sales Report": Region, Month, Revenue, Expenses. Data: ["North", "March", 125000, 98000], ["South", "March", 89000, 72000].
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Adding new rows to existing data

prompt_templates.title: Full workflow - Create, populate, and export
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new spreadsheet "Q1 Performance Review" with headers: Department, Budget, Actual, Variance. Populate with data for 5 departments. Then export the spreadsheet as an Excel file and provide the download link.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Multi-step workflow combining create, write, and export

output_examples.input: Create a spreadsheet titled "Project Budget" with headers: Category, Allocated, Spent, Remaining
output_examples.output: Spreadsheet created successfully. Title: Project Budget. URL: https://workspace.larksuite.com/sheets/shtcnXXXXXXX. Token: shtcnXXXXXXX. Ready for data input.

output_examples.input: Find all cells containing "pending" in the range A1:Z500 of spreadsheet shtcnXXXXXXX
output_examples.output: Found 12 matching cells. Locations: B15 (Status column - "pending review"), D23 (Approval - "pending manager"), H88 (Task state - "pending"). Total rows scanned: 500.

output_examples.input: Export the spreadsheet "Inventory List" as an Excel file
output_examples.output: Export task initiated. Task ID: task_xxxxx. Status: Processing. Once complete, download URL will be provided. File format: Excel (.xlsx). Estimated time: 30-60 seconds.

best_practices: Always use --dry-run first to preview CLI parameters before executing write operations
Verify spreadsheet tokens and sheet IDs before performing read or write operations to avoid data corruption
Use --range parameter to limit operations to specific cell ranges for better performance and safety
anti_patterns: Do not use sheets +find to search for spreadsheets by name - use lark-doc skill's docs +search first to locate files
Avoid writing large datasets without validation - always preview with --dry-run and confirm data structure matches sheet layout
Do not assume file_token from wiki URLs - always resolve wiki tokens to actual obj_token using the wiki API first

faq.question: How do I find a spreadsheet by name?
faq.answer: Use the lark-doc skill with docs +search first. The search results will include spreadsheet (SHEET) type documents. Then use the returned URL or token with lark-sheets operations.

faq.question: What is the difference between file_token and obj_token?
faq.answer: For most documents, the URL path token is the file_token. For wiki links, you must first query the wiki API to get the actual obj_token, as wiki URLs use a different token type.

faq.question: Can I append data without knowing the sheet_id?
faq.answer: No. Use +info first to get sheet information including available sheet_ids. The first sheet in a new spreadsheet is typically the default target.

faq.question: How do I prevent accidental data overwrites?
faq.answer: Always use --dry-run to preview write operations. For append operations, verify the range starts at the next empty row. Use +read to verify existing data before writing.

faq.question: What permissions are required to use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill requires appropriate Lark API scopes: sheets:spreadsheet:create for creating, sheets:spreadsheet.meta:read for reading metadata, and sheets:spreadsheet:write_only for write operations.

faq.question: Can I export spreadsheets to formats other than Excel?
faq.answer: The export function primarily supports Excel format. Check the Lark API documentation for additional export format options that may be available through the API.
